era_context: The mid-second millennium BCE continues with the same major powers and civilizations as 1500–1501 BCE. Egypt's New Kingdom is consolidating power under the 18th Dynasty. Mycenaean civilization is in its mature phase. The Shang dynasty controls China. The Hittite Old Kingdom is expanding in Anatolia. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its final urban phase.

events.title: Egyptian military campaigns in the Levant under Thutmose III
events.description: Thutmose III continues military campaigns to maintain Egyptian control over Levantine territories. These campaigns are part of a broader strategy to secure trade routes, suppress rebellions, and establish Egyptian hegemony in the region. The campaigns are documented in temple inscriptions at Karnak and other Egyptian sites.

sources.name: Karnak Temple inscriptions and Annals of Thutmose III

events.certainty_note: Thutmose III's campaigns are well-documented, but specific year assignment carries ±5 year uncertainty due to chronological debates.

events.title: Shang dynasty oracle bone divination continues
events.description: The Shang dynasty continues its systematic practice of oracle bone divination. Inscribed bones document royal activities, religious ceremonies, and administrative concerns. This practice provides evidence of Shang governance and cosmology.
events.certainty_note: Oracle bones are directly dated through archaeological stratigraphy. Specific year assignment carries ±50 year uncertainty.

events.title: Mycenaean palace culture at peak development
events.description: Mycenaean palace complexes continue to flourish. Architectural projects, artistic production, and administrative systems are at high levels. Linear B script is used for record-keeping. Trade networks extend throughout the Mediterranean.

sources.name: Archaeological excavations at Mycenaean palace sites
Linear B tablets from palace archives

events.certainty_note: Mycenaean palace culture is archaeologically well-attested. Specific year assignment carries ±50 year uncertainty.

events.title: Hittite Old Kingdom consolidates Anatolian power
events.description: The Hittite Old Kingdom continues to consolidate power in Anatolia through military campaigns and administrative expansion. Hittite rulers establish control over neighboring territories and city-states.
key_figures: Hittite Old Kingdom rulers (specific names uncertain)
events.certainty_note: Hittite Old Kingdom chronology carries ±50–100 year uncertainty.

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization in advanced decline phase
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues its decline. Urban centers are depopulating, architectural quality is declining, and craft production is simplified. The civilization is fragmenting into regional cultures.

sources.name: Archaeological excavations at Indus Valley sites

events.certainty_note: The decline is archaeologically documented. Specific year assignment carries ±50–100 year uncertainty.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 1502 BCE is similar to previous years in this period: heavily weighted toward literate civilizations with surviving written records. Egyptian and Hittite records are politically motivated. Mycenaean Linear B tablets are administrative. The Indus Valley Civilization's undeciphered script limits understanding. Sub-Saharan Africa, northern Europe, and the Americas are severely underrepresented.
